name: Dum Aloo (Whole Potatoes in Spicy Yogurt Gravy)
recipeIngredient: 2 cups chopped fresh tomatoes, or 1 cup canned tomatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the potatoes and prick them in 4 or 5 places. Place them in a bowl of cold water until ready to cook.
2. Heat 5 tablespoons vegetable oil in a large non-stick pan that can hold all the potatoes in one layer over medium-high heat. When the oil is very hot, drain the potatoes, pat them dry on paper towels, and add them to the pan with oil. Fry until they have several tiny browned spots and a crust (about 8-10 minutes), turning and tossing them for even browning.
3. Transfer the fried potatoes to a bowl using a slotted spoon.
4. Add the remaining oil to the pan and fry onions until caramel brown, stirring constantly so they do not burn (about 15 minutes). The onions will become sticky and the oil will separate from them.
5. Add ginger and fry for an additional 30 seconds.
6. Add cumin, coriander, turmeric, red pepper, and Mughal garam masala all at once, stirring rapidly for 15 seconds.
7. Add tomatoes, yogurt, salt, and the fried potatoes (in one layer) to the pan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer very gently, covered, for 35 minutes or until the potatoes are fully cooked. Check during cooking to ensure the gravy is not sticking and burning.
8. If the gravy looks thin and runny, increase heat and boil rapidly, uncovered, until it reduces to the desired consistency. If the gravy is too thick, add a few tablespoons of water.
9. Add cream, stir, and simmer until heated through. Stir in a little more oil (about 2 tablespoons) for a milder flavor if desired. Check for salt and serve.
Note: This dish improves with keeping. For best results, make it at least a few hours before serving and refrigerate up to 4 days without loss of flavor.
